name: Tatarstan (Russia)

colors.symbolism: spring, renewal, and the Tatar people; also associated with Islam
peace, purity, and harmony between the republic's peoples
maturity, strength, vitality, and the Russian people

description: A horizontal triband of green over white over red, with the central white stripe much narrower than the outer two.
history: Adopted on 29 November 1991 by the Supreme Council of the Republic of Tatarstan, designed by Tavil Khaziakhmetov. The proportions of the central white stripe are precisely 1/15 of the flag's total width.

features.symbolism: the green and red bands represent the Tatar people and their territory; the narrow white stripe between them represents peace and harmony between the peoples of Tatarstan.
